hints: A ? placeholder is a slot for a VALUE. A column name is part of the query's structure, not a value — so it can't be parameterized, and the safe way to pick from user input is an allowlist of known-good names.
For the matching: exactly one defense removes the hole (the one that keeps input out of the code path). The others don't stop the attack — they shrink what a successful attack can reach or reveal.
Least privilege limits what a compromised query can touch; safe error handling stops leaked messages from mapping your schema; allowlist validation is the identifier answer from the first question.
